sections.title: ACP Internals
sections.summary: Documents the ACP (Agent Client Protocol) adapter that wraps Hermes' synchronous AIAgent in an async JSON-RPC stdio server for VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains, covering boot flow, SessionManager, event bridge, permission bridge, session lifecycle, cancelation, and forking.

sections.title: Adding a Platform Adapter
sections.summary: Complete guide for adding a new messaging platform adapter to the Hermes gateway, covering the recommended plugin path (PLUGIN.yaml + adapter.py with ctx.register_platform()) and the built-in core path (20+ file checklist across gateway, CLI, tools, and docs).

sections.title: Adding Providers
sections.summary: Comprehensive guide for adding a new inference provider to Hermes, covering two paths (OpenAI-compatible plugin vs. native provider), file checklists, auth.py, models.py, runtime_provider.py, main.py wiring, auxiliary client defaults, and common pitfalls.

sections.title: Adding Tools
sections.summary: Step-by-step guide for adding a new built-in Hermes tool to the repository, covering tool file structure, schema definition, registry.register() call, toolset assignment, async handlers, agent-loop intercepted tools, and setup wizard integration.

sections.title: Agent Loop Internals
sections.summary: Deep dive into the AIAgent class in run_agent.py covering the turn lifecycle, API modes (chat_completions / codex_responses / anthropic_messages), interruptible API calls, tool execution (sequential vs concurrent), callbacks, iteration budget, fallback behavior, and compression/persistence.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.summary: Top-level map of Hermes Agent internals covering directory structure, major subsystems (agent loop, prompt system, provider resolution, tool system, session persistence, gateway, plugin system, cron, ACP, trajectories), data flow, and design principles.

sections.title: Browser CDP Supervisor
sections.summary: Design document for the Browser CDP Supervisor (PR 14540) that detects and handles native JS dialogs and cross-origin iframes, including the CDPSupervisor architecture, dialog policy configuration, browser_dialog tool, browser_snapshot extensions, and OOPIF interaction via frame_id routing.

sections.title: Context Compression and Caching
sections.summary: Documents the dual compression system (gateway hygiene at 85% threshold and agent ContextCompressor at 50% threshold), the 4-phase compression algorithm, Anthropic prompt caching strategy, and the pluggable ContextEngine ABC.

sections.title: Context Engine Plugins
sections.summary: Guide for building context engine plugins that replace the built-in ContextCompressor with alternative context management strategies, covering the ContextEngine ABC required methods, optional hooks, engine tool registration, lifecycle, and registration via directory or plugin system.

sections.title: Contributing
sections.summary: Guide for setting up a development environment, understanding code style, cross-platform compatibility rules, and the pull request process for contributing to Hermes Agent.

sections.title: Creating Skills
sections.summary: Complete guide for creating Hermes skills — the preferred way to add capabilities without code changes — covering SKILL.md format, frontmatter fields, platform restrictions, conditional activation (requires_toolsets, fallback_for_tools), environment variable requirements, credential file requirements, helper scripts, and publishing.

sections.title: Cron Internals
sections.summary: Documents the cron subsystem covering scheduling model (4 formats), job storage in jobs.json, scheduler tick cycle, skill/script injection, provider recovery with fallback support, delivery model across all platforms, recursion guard, cross-process locking, and CLI interface.

sections.title: Extending the CLI
sections.summary: Documents the five extension hooks on HermesCLI for building wrapper CLIs that add custom widgets, keybindings, layout customizations, slash commands, and styles without overriding the main run() method.

sections.title: Gateway Internals
sections.summary: Documents the messaging gateway architecture covering GatewayRunner, message flow, session key format, two-level message guard, user authorization (allowlists + DM pairing), slash command dispatch, platform adapters (20+), delivery paths, hooks, and background maintenance tasks.

sections.title: Image Generation Provider Plugins
sections.summary: Guide for building image generation provider plugins using the ImageGenProvider ABC, covering discovery, directory structure, required generate() method, success_response/error_response format, base64 vs URL handling, user overrides, and distribution via pip.

sections.title: Memory Provider Plugins
sections.summary: Guide for building memory provider plugins that give Hermes Agent persistent cross-session knowledge, covering the MemoryProvider ABC lifecycle methods, config schema, threading contract for sync_turn(), profile isolation, CLI command registration, and the single-provider rule.

sections.title: Model Provider Plugins
sections.summary: Guide for building model provider plugins using ProviderProfile, covering discovery order, auto-wire integrations (auth, CLI, model picker, doctor, setup wizard, runtime resolution), ProviderProfile field reference, overridable hooks (prepare_messages, build_extra_body, fetch_models), api_mode selection, auth types, and user overrides.

sections.title: Plugin LLM Access
sections.summary: Documents ctx.llm, the API for plugins to make out-of-band LLM calls using the host's credentials, covering complete(), complete_structured(), async variants (acomplete/acomplete_structured), structured output with JSON schema, trust gate configuration, and result attributes.

sections.title: Programmatic Integration
sections.summary: Documents the three protocols for driving Hermes from external programs: ACP (JSON-RPC stdio for IDE clients), TUI Gateway JSON-RPC (custom hosts with full feature access), and the OpenAI-compatible API server (HTTP+SSE for web frontends and curl-driven automation).

sections.title: Prompt Assembly
sections.summary: Explains how Hermes separates cached system prompt state from ephemeral API-call-time additions, the 10-layer prompt assembly order (SOUL.md, memory, skills, context files, etc.), context file priority system, and when to edit prompt_builder.py vs using supported customization surfaces.

sections.title: Provider Runtime Resolution
sections.summary: Documents the shared provider runtime resolver used across CLI, gateway, cron, ACP, and auxiliary calls — covering resolution precedence, supported provider families, API modes, fallback model activation, and auxiliary model routing.

sections.title: Session Storage
sections.summary: Documents the SQLite-based session storage system (state.db) covering schema design (sessions, messages, FTS5 tables), schema migrations, write contention handling with retry/jitter, full-text search capabilities, session lineage via parent_session_id, and common operations.

sections.title: Tools Runtime
sections.summary: Documents the tool registration model, auto-discovery via discover_builtin_tools(), check_fn availability checking, toolset resolution, dispatch flow from model tool_call to handler execution, dangerous command approval system, async bridging, and terminal/runtime environments.

sections.title: Trajectory Format
sections.summary: Documents the ShareGPT-compatible JSONL trajectory format used for training data, debugging, and RL datasets, covering file naming convention, two JSONL entry variants, conversations array format, normalization rules for reasoning content and tool calls, loading utilities, and how to control trajectory saving.

sections.title: Video Generation Provider Plugins
sections.summary: Guide for building video generation provider plugins using the VideoGenProvider ABC, covering the unified text-to-video + image-to-video surface, capabilities() method, model families and endpoint routing (FAL pattern), selection precedence, success_response/error_response format, and artifact saving.

sections.title: Hermes Agent Documentation
sections.summary: The self-improving AI agent built by Nous Research(https://nousresearch.com). The only agent with a built-in learning loop — it creates skills from experience, improves them during use, nudges itself to persist knowledge, and builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions.

sections.title: Installation
sections.summary: Covers all installation methods for Hermes Agent including one-line curl installer for Linux/macOS/WSL2, Windows PowerShell installer, Android/Termux path, pip install, and non-sudo/system service user installs.

sections.title: Learning Path
sections.summary: Structured reading guide organized by experience level (beginner/intermediate/advanced) and use case (CLI assistant, bot, automation, custom tools, RL training, Python library) with feature overview table.

sections.title: Nix & NixOS Setup
sections.summary: Full guide to installing and running Hermes Agent via Nix flake: nix run/profile install for any Nix user, NixOS module for declarative server deployments (native and container modes), secrets management with sops-nix/agenix, MCP server config, plugin installation, and a complete options reference.

sections.title: Quickstart
sections.summary: Step-by-step guide from zero to a working Hermes setup: install, choose an LLM provider, run a first chat, verify sessions, and add optional layers like gateway bots, voice, skills, and MCP servers.

sections.title: Android / Termux
sections.summary: Tested installation guide for running Hermes Agent on Android via Termux, covering the one-line installer, manual install steps, supported extras, known limitations, and Android-specific troubleshooting.

sections.title: Updating & Uninstalling
sections.summary: Complete guide to updating Hermes Agent (git, pip, manual, and from messaging platforms), post-update validation, rollback procedures, and uninstallation for all install methods.

sections.title: Automate Anything with Cron
sections.summary: Five real-world automation patterns for Hermes cron jobs: website change monitor with state tracking, weekly report generation, GitHub repository watcher, data collection pipeline with script+agent split, and multi-skill chained workflows.

sections.title: Automation Templates
sections.summary: Copy-paste recipes for common automation patterns. Each template uses Hermes's built-in [cron scheduler](/docs/user-guide/features/cron) for time-based triggers and [webhook platform](/docs/user-guide/messaging/webhooks) for event-driven triggers. Every template works with **any model** — not loc...

sections.title: AWS Bedrock
sections.summary: Hermes Agent supports Amazon Bedrock as a native provider using the **Converse API** — not the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. This gives you full access to the Bedrock ecosystem: IAM authentication, Guardrails, cross-region inference profiles, and all foundation models. - **AWS credentials** — any s...

sections.title: Azure AI Foundry
sections.summary: Hermes Agent supports Azure AI Foundry (and Azure OpenAI) as a first-class provider. A single Azure resource can host models with two different wire formats: - **OpenAI-style** — `POST /v1/chat/completions` on endpoints like `https://<resource>.openai.azure.com/openai/v1`. Used for GPT-4.x, GPT-5...

sections.title: Build a Hermes Plugin
sections.summary: Complete step-by-step guide for building a Hermes plugin with custom tools, lifecycle hooks, slash commands, CLI subcommands, bundled skills, and data files, covering plugin.yaml manifest, tool schemas, handler functions, and the registration API.

sections.title: Script-Only Cron Jobs (No LLM)
sections.summary: Sometimes you already know exactly what message you want to send. You don't need an agent to reason about it — you just need a script to run on a timer, and its output (if any) to land in Telegram / Discord / Slack / Signal. Hermes calls this **no-agent mode**. It's the cron system minus the LLM.

sections.title: Cron Troubleshooting
sections.summary: When a cron job isn't behaving as expected, work through these checks in order. Most issues fall into one of four categories: timing, delivery, permissions, or skill loading. ---

sections.title: Tutorial: Daily Briefing Bot
sections.summary: Step-by-step tutorial for building a personal automated morning briefing bot that uses web search, cron scheduling, and delegation to research topics and deliver a formatted summary to Telegram or Discord each morning.

sections.title: Delegation & Parallel Work
sections.summary: Practical patterns for using Hermes subagent delegation effectively: parallel research, code review in isolated context, comparing alternatives, multi-file refactoring, gather-then-analyze split, toolset selection, and concurrency/depth configuration.

sections.title: Tutorial: GitHub PR Review Agent
sections.summary: Tutorial for building an automated GitHub PR review agent using Hermes cron jobs, the GitHub CLI (gh), a custom code-review skill, and memory-stored team conventions, with support for Telegram/Discord delivery and optional direct GitHub PR comment posting.

sections.title: Google Gemini
sections.summary: Hermes Agent supports Google Gemini as a native provider using the **Google AI Studio / Gemini API** — not the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. This lets Hermes translate its internal OpenAI-shaped message and tool loop into Gemini's native `generateContent` API while preserving tool calling, streamin...

sections.title: Run Local LLMs on Mac
sections.summary: Step-by-step guide for running local LLM servers on macOS with an OpenAI-compatible API using either llama.cpp (fastest time-to-first-token via Metal) or MLX via omlx (fastest token generation), then connecting Hermes to the local endpoint.

sections.title: Run Hermes Locally with Ollama — Zero API Cost
sections.summary: Cloud LLM APIs charge per token. A heavy coding session can cost $5–20. For personal projects, learning, or privacy-sensitive work, that adds up — and you're sending every conversation to a third party. You'll set up Hermes Agent running entirely on your own hardware, using [Ollama](https://ollam...

sections.title: Register a Microsoft Graph Application
sections.summary: The Teams meeting pipeline reads meeting transcripts, recordings, and related artifacts from Microsoft Graph using **app-only** (daemon) authentication — no user sign-in, no interactive consent per meeting. That requires an Azure AD application registration with admin-consented application permis...

sections.title: Migrate from OpenClaw
sections.summary: hermes claw migrate hermes claw migrate --dry-run

sections.title: MiniMax OAuth
sections.summary: Hermes Agent supports **MiniMax** through a browser-based OAuth login flow, using the same credentials as the [MiniMax portal](https://www.minimax.io). No API key or credit card is required — log in once and Hermes automatically refreshes your session. The transport reuses the `anthropic_messages...

sections.title: OAuth over SSH / Remote Hosts
sections.summary: Some Hermes providers — currently **xAI Grok OAuth** and **Spotify** — use a *loopback redirect* OAuth flow. The auth server (xAI, Spotify) redirects your browser to `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback` so a tiny HTTP listener started by the `hermes auth ...` command can grab the authorization code...

sections.title: Operate the Teams Meeting Pipeline
sections.summary: Use this guide after you have already enabled the feature from [Teams Meetings](/docs/user-guide/messaging/teams-meetings). This page covers:

sections.title: Using Hermes as a Python Library
sections.summary: How to import and use Hermes Agent's AIAgent class programmatically in Python scripts, web applications, and automation pipelines, including multi-turn conversations, toolset control, trajectory saving, custom system prompts, and batch processing.

sections.title: Tutorial: Team Telegram Assistant
sections.summary: Complete walkthrough for setting up a Telegram bot powered by Hermes Agent for multiple team members, covering BotFather setup, gateway configuration, team access via static allowlists or DM pairing, scheduled tasks, and production hardening.

sections.title: Use MCP with Hermes
sections.summary: Practical guide for using MCP with Hermes Agent day-to-day: when to use MCP vs built-in tools, step-by-step setup, filtering best practices, WSL2 browser bridging, common patterns (project assistant, GitHub triage, internal API, docs), and troubleshooting by symptom.

sections.title: Use SOUL.md with Hermes
sections.summary: How to use SOUL.md to define the primary identity and personality for your Hermes instance — tone, communication style, and behavioral defaults — vs. AGENTS.md which handles project-specific instructions.

sections.title: Use Voice Mode with Hermes
sections.summary: Practical guide for setting up and using all three Hermes voice modes: CLI microphone loop, voice replies in Telegram/Discord messaging, and Discord live voice channel bot, with provider recommendations, config tuning, and troubleshooting.

sections.title: Automated GitHub PR Comments with Webhooks
sections.summary: This guide walks you through connecting Hermes Agent to GitHub so it automatically fetches a pull request's diff, analyzes the code changes, and posts a comment — triggered by a webhook event with no manual prompting. When a PR is opened or updated, GitHub sends a webhook POST to your Hermes inst...

sections.title: Working with Skills
sections.summary: Practical guide to using, installing, creating, and managing Hermes skills — on-demand knowledge documents that teach Hermes how to handle specific tasks — covering progressive disclosure loading, the Skills Hub, plugin-provided skills, and the skills-vs-memory decision.

sections.title: xAI Grok OAuth (SuperGrok Subscription)
sections.summary: Hermes Agent supports xAI Grok through a browser-based OAuth login flow against [accounts.x.ai](https://accounts.x.ai), using your existing **SuperGrok subscription**. No `XAI_API_KEY` is required — log in once and Hermes automatically refreshes your session in the background. The transport reuse...

sections.title: Tips & Best Practices
sections.summary: A collection of practical quick-wins for getting the best results from Hermes Agent, covering prompt writing, CLI power user features, context file usage, memory vs. skills, performance optimization, messaging tips, and security practices.

sections.title: Integrations
sections.summary: Overview of all Hermes Agent integration points: AI providers, MCP tool servers, web search backends, browser automation, voice/TTS providers, IDE integration via ACP, API server, memory backends, and 19+ messaging platforms.

sections.title: AI Providers
sections.summary: Complete reference for configuring LLM inference providers in Hermes Agent, including cloud APIs (OpenRouter, Anthropic, GitHub Copilot, Google Gemini), self-hosted endpoints (Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, LM Studio), and advanced routing/fallback options.

sections.title: CLI Commands Reference
sections.summary: This page covers the **terminal commands** you run from your shell. For in-chat slash commands, see [Slash Commands Reference](./slash-commands.md).

sections.title: Environment Variables
sections.summary: All variables go in `~/.hermes/.env`. You can also set them with `hermes config set VAR value`. For native Anthropic auth, Hermes prefers Claude Code's own credential files when they exist because those credentials can refresh automatically. **OAuth against Anthropic requires a Claude Max plan wi...

sections.title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
sections.summary: Quick answers and fixes for the most common questions and issues. ---

sections.title: MCP Config Reference
sections.summary: This page is the compact reference companion to the main MCP docs. For conceptual guidance, see:

sections.title: reference/model-catalog
sections.summary: Hermes fetches curated model lists for **OpenRouter** and **Nous Portal** from a JSON manifest hosted alongside the docs site. This lets maintainers update picker lists without shipping a new `hermes-agent` release. When the manifest is unreachable (offline, network blocked, hosting failure), Her...

sections.title: Optional Skills Catalog
sections.summary: Optional skills ship with hermes-agent under `optional-skills/` but are **not active by default**. Install them explicitly: ```bash

sections.title: reference/profile-commands
sections.summary: This page covers all commands related to [Hermes profiles](../user-guide/profiles.md). For general CLI commands, see [CLI Commands Reference](./cli-commands.md). hermes profile <subcommand>

sections.title: Bundled Skills Catalog
sections.summary: Hermes ships with a large built-in skill library copied into `~/.hermes/skills/` on install. Each skill below links to a dedicated page with its full definition, setup, and usage. Hermes also syncs bundled skills on `hermes update`, but the sync manifest respects local deletions and user edits. I...

sections.title: Slash Commands Reference
sections.summary: Hermes has two slash-command surfaces, both driven by a central `COMMAND_REGISTRY` in `hermes_cli/commands.py`: - **Interactive CLI slash commands** — dispatched by `cli.py`, with autocomplete from the registry

sections.title: Built-in Tools Reference
sections.summary: This page documents Hermes' built-in tools, grouped by toolset. Availability varies by platform, credentials, and enabled toolsets. **Quick counts (current registry):** ~70 tools — 10 browser tools (core) + 2 CDP-gated browser tools, 4 file tools, 10 RL tools, 4 Home Assistant tools, 2 terminal t...

sections.title: Toolsets Reference
sections.summary: Toolsets are named bundles of tools that control what the agent can do. They're the primary mechanism for configuring tool availability per platform, per session, or per task. Every tool belongs to exactly one toolset. When you enable a toolset, all tools in that bundle become available to the ag...

sections.title: Checkpoints and /rollback
sections.summary: Explains Hermes's opt-in checkpoint system that automatically snapshots project files before destructive operations, with /rollback for restore, single-file restore, and storage management.

sections.title: CLI Interface
sections.summary: Documents Hermes Agent's full-featured terminal CLI, including keybindings, slash commands, session management, background sessions, and busy-input modes.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.summary: Complete reference for all Hermes configuration settings including terminal backends, compression, TTS, display, web search backends, security, approvals, and context files.

sections.title: Configuring Models
sections.summary: Explains how to configure the main model and auxiliary models (vision, compression, session search, etc.) via the dashboard, CLI slash commands, model aliases, and REST API.

sections.title: Hermes Agent — Docker
sections.summary: Covers running Hermes Agent itself inside Docker — gateway mode, dashboard, persistent volumes, multi-profile setup, resource limits, and connecting to local inference servers.

sections.title: Git Worktrees
sections.summary: Explains how to use Git worktrees with Hermes to run multiple agents in parallel on the same repo without interference, including the automatic -w worktree mode.

sections.title: user-guide/profile-distributions.md
sections.summary: A profile distribution packages a complete Hermes agent — personality, skills, cron jobs, MCP connections, config — as a git repository. Anyone with access to the repo can install the whole agent with one command, update it in place, and keep their own memories, sessions, and API keys untouched.

sections.title: Profiles: Running Multiple Agents
sections.summary: Explains how to create and manage multiple independent Hermes agent profiles, each with its own config, keys, memory, sessions, and gateway, plus profile distributions for sharing agents.

sections.title: Security
sections.summary: Comprehensive security reference covering command approval modes, hardline blocklist, YOLO mode, user authorization, DM pairing, container isolation, MCP credential filtering, SSRF protection, and context file injection protection.

sections.title: Sessions
sections.summary: Explains how Hermes stores, names, resumes, searches, and prunes sessions across CLI and messaging platforms, including cross-platform handoff and auto-lineage on compression.

sections.title: TUI
sections.summary: Covers Hermes's modern terminal UI (TUI) built on Node.js, with rich overlays, non-blocking input, LaTeX rendering, light-terminal detection, and live session status.

sections.title: Windows (Native) Guide — Early Beta
sections.summary: Hermes runs natively on Windows 10 and Windows 11 — no WSL, no Cygwin, no Docker. This page is the deep dive: what works natively, what's WSL-only, what the installer actually does, and the Windows-specific knobs you might need to touch.

sections.title: Windows (WSL2) Guide
sections.summary: Hermes Agent now supports both native Windows and WSL2. This page covers the WSL2 path; for the native PowerShell install see the dedicated Windows (Native) Guide(./windows-native.md).

sections.title: ACP Editor Integration
sections.summary: How to run Hermes Agent as an ACP (Agent Client Protocol) server for use inside ACP-compatible editors like VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains, enabling editor-native chat, file diffs, tool activity, and terminal commands.

sections.title: API Server
sections.summary: How to expose Hermes Agent as an OpenAI-compatible HTTP API server (port 8642) supporting Chat Completions, Responses API, Runs API, and Jobs API, enabling frontends like Open WebUI, LobeChat, and LibreChat to use Hermes as a full-toolset backend.

sections.title: Batch Processing
sections.summary: Batch processing runs the Hermes agent across thousands of prompts in parallel to generate ShareGPT-format training trajectories with tool usage statistics and reasoning coverage metrics.

sections.title: Browser Automation
sections.summary: Provides full browser automation through multiple backends (Browserbase, Browser Use, Firecrawl, Camofox, local Chrome via CDP) with accessibility-tree-based page interaction and tools for navigation, clicking, typing, vision analysis, and raw CDP access.

sections.title: Built-in Plugins
sections.summary: Hermes ships a small set of plugins bundled with the repository. They live under `<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and load automatically alongside user-installed plugins in `~/.hermes/plugins/`.

sections.title: Code Execution
sections.summary: The `execute_code` tool lets the agent write Python scripts that call Hermes tools programmatically, collapsing multi-step workflows into a single LLM turn. The script runs in a child process on the agent host, communicating with Hermes over a Unix domain socket RPC.

sections.title: user-guide/features/codex-app-server-runtime.md
sections.summary: Hermes can optionally hand openai/ and openai-codex/ turns to the Codex CLI app-server(https://github.com/openai/codex) instead of running its own tool loop. When enabled, terminal commands, file edits, sandboxing, and MCP tool calls all execute inside Codex's runtime — Hermes becomes the shell around it (sessions DB, slash commands, gateway, memory and skill review).

sections.title: user-guide/features/computer-use.md
sections.summary: Hermes Agent can drive your Mac's desktop — clicking, typing, scrolling, dragging — in the background. Your cursor doesn't move, keyboard focus doesn't change, and macOS doesn't switch Spaces on you.

sections.title: Context Files
sections.summary: Hermes Agent automatically discovers and loads context files that shape how it behaves. Some are project-local and discovered from your working directory.

sections.title: Context References
sections.summary: Type `@` followed by a reference to inject content directly into your message. Hermes expands the reference inline and appends the content under an `--- Attached Context ---` section.

sections.title: Credential Pools
sections.summary: Credential pools let you register multiple API keys or OAuth tokens for the same provider; Hermes automatically rotates to the next healthy key when one hits rate limits or billing quotas, providing same-provider key rotation before cross-provider fallback activates.

sections.title: Scheduled Tasks (Cron)
sections.summary: Schedule tasks to run automatically with natural language or cron expressions. Hermes exposes cron management through a single `cronjob` tool with action-style operations instead of separate schedule/list/remove tools.

sections.title: Curator
sections.summary: The curator is a background maintenance pass for **agent-created skills**. It tracks how often each skill is viewed, used, and patched, moves long-unused skills through `active → stale → archived` states, and periodically spawns a short auxiliary-model review that proposes consolidations or patches drift.

sections.title: Subagent Delegation
sections.summary: The `delegate_task` tool spawns child AIAgent instances with isolated context, restricted toolsets, and their own terminal sessions. Each child gets a fresh conversation and works independently — only its final summary enters the parent's context.

sections.title: Extending the Dashboard
sections.summary: The Hermes web dashboard (hermes dashboard) is built to be reskinned and extended without forking the codebase. Three layers are exposed:

sections.title: Fallback Providers
sections.summary: Three-layer resilience system for Hermes: credential pools (same-provider key rotation), primary model fallback (cross-provider automatic failover on errors), and auxiliary task fallback (independent provider resolution for vision, compression, web extraction, etc.).

sections.title: Features Overview
sections.summary: Hermes Agent includes a rich set of capabilities that extend far beyond basic chat. From persistent memory and file-aware context to browser automation and voice conversations, these features work together to make Hermes a powerful autonomous assistant.

sections.title: Persistent Goals
sections.summary: `/goal` gives Hermes a standing objective that survives across turns. After every turn a lightweight judge model checks whether the goal is satisfied by the assistant's last response.

sections.title: Honcho Memory
sections.summary: Honcho is an AI-native memory provider plugin that adds dialectic reasoning and deep user modeling to Hermes, maintaining a running model of user preferences, communication style, and goals through LLM-synthesized reasoning across sessions.

sections.title: Event Hooks
sections.summary: Hermes has three hook systems that run custom code at key lifecycle points: All three systems are non-blocking — errors in any hook are caught and logged, never crashing the agent.

sections.title: Image Generation
sections.summary: Generates images from text prompts via FAL.ai with nine selectable models ranging from sub-second fast generators to SOTA typography and photorealism models; model selection, aspect ratios, and upscaling are all configurable.

sections.title: Kanban (Multi-Agent Board)
sections.summary: > **Want a walkthrough?** Read the [Kanban tutorial](./kanban-tutorial) — four user stories (solo dev, fleet farming, role pipeline with retry, circuit breaker) with dashboard screenshots of each. This page is the reference; the tutorial is the narrative.

sections.title: Kanban Tutorial
sections.summary: A walkthrough of the four use-cases the Hermes Kanban system was designed for, with the dashboard open in a browser. If you haven't read the [Kanban overview](./kanban) yet, start there — this assumes you know what a task, run, assignee, and dispatcher are.

sections.title: user-guide/features/kanban-worker-lanes.md
sections.summary: A worker lane is a class of process that the kanban dispatcher can route tasks to. Each lane has an identity (the assignee string), a spawn mechanism, and a contract for what it must do with the task once spawned.

sections.title: LSP — Semantic Diagnostics
sections.summary: Hermes runs full language servers — pyright, gopls, rust-analyzer, typescript-language-server, clangd, and ~20 more — as background subprocesses and feeds their semantic diagnostics into the post-write lint check used by write_file and patch. When the agent edits a file, it sees exactly the errors that edit introduced — not just syntax errors, but type errors, undefined names, missing imports, and

sections.title: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
sections.summary: How to connect Hermes Agent to external tool servers via Model Context Protocol (MCP), including stdio and HTTP server types, per-server tool filtering, dynamic tool discovery, MCP sampling support, and running Hermes itself as an MCP server.

sections.title: Persistent Memory
sections.summary: Hermes Agent has bounded, curated memory that persists across sessions. This lets it remember your preferences, your projects, your environment, and things it has learned.

sections.title: Memory Providers
sections.summary: Hermes Agent ships with 8 external memory provider plugins that give the agent persistent, cross-session knowledge beyond the built-in MEMORY.md and USER.md. Only **one** external provider can be active at a time — the built-in memory is always active alongside it.

sections.title: Personality & SOUL.md
sections.summary: Hermes Agent's personality is fully customizable. `SOUL.md` is the **primary identity** — it's the first thing in the system prompt and defines who the agent is.

sections.title: Plugins
sections.summary: Hermes has a plugin system for adding custom tools, hooks, and integrations without modifying core code. If you want to create a custom tool for yourself, your team, or one project,

sections.title: Provider Routing
sections.summary: How to configure fine-grained control over which underlying providers handle OpenRouter requests in Hermes, including sorting by cost/speed/quality, provider whitelists/blacklists, explicit ordering, parameter requirements, and data collection policies.

sections.title: Skills System
sections.summary: Skills are on-demand knowledge documents the agent can load when needed. They follow a **progressive disclosure** pattern to minimize token usage and are compatible with the [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io/specification) open standard.

sections.title: Skins & Themes
sections.summary: Skins control the visual presentation of the Hermes CLI: banner colors, spinner faces and verbs, response-box labels, branding text, and the tool activity prefix.

sections.title: user-guide/features/spotify.md
sections.summary: Hermes can control Spotify directly — playback, queue, search, playlists, saved tracks/albums, and listening history — using Spotify's official Web API with PKCE OAuth. Tokens are stored in ~/.hermes/auth.json and refreshed automatically on 401; you only log in once per machine.

sections.title: Subscription Proxy
sections.summary: The subscription proxy is a local HTTP server that lets external apps — OpenViking, Karakeep, Open WebUI, anything that speaks OpenAI-compatible chat completions — use your Hermes-managed provider subscription as their LLM endpoint. The proxy attaches the right credentials (refreshing them automatically) so the app never needs a static API key.

sections.title: Nous Tool Gateway
sections.summary: One subscription. Every tool built in.

sections.title: Tools & Toolsets
sections.summary: Tools are functions that extend the agent's capabilities. They're organized into logical **toolsets** that can be enabled or disabled per platform.

sections.title: Voice & TTS
sections.summary: Configures text-to-speech output (10 providers including Edge TTS free, ElevenLabs, OpenAI, MiniMax, Gemini, xAI, Piper, and custom command providers) and automatic voice message transcription (STT) across all messaging platforms.

sections.title: Vision & Image Paste
sections.summary: Enables multimodal vision by letting users paste images from clipboard into the CLI or messaging platforms; automatically routes images to the model as native vision content or through an auxiliary vision tool for text-only models.

sections.title: Voice Mode
sections.summary: Enables full voice interaction in the CLI (push-to-talk with Ctrl+B) and messaging platforms (Telegram/Discord voice replies and Discord voice channel conversations) using STT and TTS providers.

sections.title: Web Dashboard
sections.summary: The web dashboard is a browser-based UI for managing your Hermes Agent installation. Instead of editing YAML files or running CLI commands, you can configure settings, manage API keys, and monitor sessions from a clean web interface.

sections.title: user-guide/features/web-search.md
sections.summary: Hermes Agent includes two model-callable web tools backed by multiple providers:

sections.title: user-guide/messaging/bluebubbles.md
sections.summary: Connect Hermes to Apple iMessage via BlueBubbles(https://bluebubbles.app/) — a free, open-source macOS server that bridges iMessage to any device.

sections.title: DingTalk
sections.summary: Hermes Agent integrates with DingTalk (钉钉) as a chatbot, letting you chat with your AI assistant through direct messages or group chats. The bot connects via DingTalk's Stream Mode — a long-lived WebSocket connection that requires no public URL or webhook server — and replies using markdown-formatted messages through DingTalk's session webhook API.

sections.title: Discord Integration
sections.summary: Discord bot integration covering Developer Portal setup, privileged intents, per-user session isolation, slash command ACL via allow_admin_from, auto-threading, forum channels, media handling, history backfill, and mention control.

sections.title: Email Integration
sections.summary: Email integration via IMAP (receive) and SMTP (send), covering Gmail app password setup, email threading headers, attachment handling, poll interval configuration, and anti-spam filtering by sender allowlist.

sections.title: Feishu / Lark
sections.summary: Hermes Agent integrates with Feishu and Lark as a full-featured bot. Once connected, you can chat with the agent in direct messages or group chats, receive cron job results in a home chat, and send text, images, audio, and file attachments through the normal gateway flow.

sections.title: Google Chat
sections.summary: Connect Hermes Agent to Google Chat as a bot. The integration uses Cloud Pub/Sub pull subscriptions for inbound events and the Chat REST API for outbound messages.

sections.title: Home Assistant Integration
sections.summary: Home Assistant integration via WebSocket API, covering state_changed event subscriptions, 4 LLM-callable HA tools (get_state, call_service, get_history, list_entities), event filtering, blocked service domains for safety, and persistent notification delivery.

sections.title: LINE
sections.summary: Run Hermes Agent as a LINE(https://line.me/) bot via the official LINE Messaging API. The adapter lives as a bundled platform plugin under plugins/platforms/line/ — no core edits, just enable it like any other platform.

sections.title: Matrix Integration
sections.summary: Matrix protocol integration via the mautrix SDK, covering account registration, E2EE with libolm, crypto store setup, cross-signing recovery key, proxy mode for macOS, MSC3245 voice messages, and room allowlisting.

sections.title: Mattermost Integration
sections.summary: Mattermost integration via REST API v4 and WebSocket, covering bot account creation, team/channel configuration, thread vs flat reply modes, mention behavior, per-channel system prompt overrides, and self-hosted Slack alternative positioning.

sections.title: Microsoft Graph Webhook Listener
sections.summary: The msgraph_webhook gateway platform is an inbound event listener. It's how Hermes receives change notifications from Microsoft Graph — "a Teams meeting ended," "a new message landed in this chat," "this calendar event was updated." Different from the teams platform (which is a chat bot users type to) — this one is M365 telling Hermes something happened, not a person.

sections.title: Open WebUI
sections.summary: Open WebUI(https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui) (126k★) is the most popular self-hosted chat interface for AI. With Hermes Agent's built-in API server, you can use Open WebUI as a polished web frontend for your agent — complete with conversation management, user accounts, and a modern chat interface.

sections.title: Messaging Gateway
sections.summary: The messaging gateway connects Hermes to 20+ platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, SMS, Email, Matrix, etc.) through a single background process that handles sessions, security, cron jobs, and voice delivery.

sections.title: user-guide/messaging/qqbot.md
sections.summary: Connect Hermes to QQ via the Official QQ Bot API (v2) — supporting private (C2C), group @-mentions, guild, and direct messages with voice transcription.

sections.title: Signal Integration
sections.summary: Signal integration via signal-cli daemon in HTTP mode, covering daemon setup, SSE event streaming, JSON-RPC API, native bodyRanges text formatting, Note-to-Self pairing, E.164 phone number allowlisting, and health monitoring.

sections.title: user-guide/messaging/simplex.md
sections.summary: SimpleX Chat(https://simplex.chat/) is a private, decentralised messaging platform where users own their contacts and groups. Unlike other platforms, SimpleX assigns no persistent user IDs — every contact is identified by an opaque internal ID generated at connection time, which makes it one of the most private messengers available.

sections.title: Slack Integration
sections.summary: Slack integration using Socket Mode and the Bolt SDK, covering app manifest generation, required bot scopes, event subscriptions, Messages Tab, multi-workspace support, thread-command prefix, and per-channel prompt and skill bindings.

sections.title: SMS Integration (Twilio)
sections.summary: SMS integration via Twilio, covering webhook HMAC-SHA1 validation, required SMS_WEBHOOK_URL config, 1600 character message length limit, plain text only (no markdown), phone number allowlisting in E.164 format, and multi-part message handling.

sections.title: Microsoft Teams
sections.summary: Connect Hermes Agent to Microsoft Teams as a bot. Unlike Slack's Socket Mode, Teams delivers messages by calling a public HTTPS webhook, so your instance needs a publicly reachable endpoint — either a dev tunnel (local dev) or a real domain (production).

sections.title: Teams Meetings
sections.summary: Use the Teams meeting pipeline when you want Hermes to ingest Microsoft Graph meeting events, fetch transcripts first, fall back to recordings plus STT when needed, and deliver a structured summary to downstream sinks.

sections.title: Telegram Integration
sections.summary: Complete guide to Telegram bot integration including BotFather setup, privacy mode, webhook vs polling, group forum topics, per-chat sessions, streaming via draft/edit transport, slash command ACL, reactions, per-channel prompts, and DNS fallback configuration.

sections.title: Webhooks Integration
sections.summary: Webhooks integration covering HMAC signature validation for GitHub/GitLab/generic sources, route configuration with prompt templates, direct delivery mode (deliver_only, zero LLM tokens), dynamic subscriptions, rate limiting, and idempotency via event ID deduplication.

sections.title: WeCom (Enterprise WeChat)
sections.summary: Connect Hermes to WeCom(https://work.weixin.qq.com/) (企业微信), Tencent's enterprise messaging platform. The adapter uses WeCom's AI Bot WebSocket gateway for real-time bidirectional communication — no public endpoint or webhook needed.

sections.title: user-guide/messaging/wecom-callback.md
sections.summary: Connect Hermes to WeCom (Enterprise WeChat) as a self-built enterprise application using the callback/webhook model.

sections.title: Weixin (WeChat)
sections.summary: Connect Hermes to WeChat(https://weixin.qq.com/) (微信), Tencent's personal messaging platform. The adapter uses Tencent's iLink Bot API for personal WeChat accounts — this is distinct from WeCom (Enterprise WeChat).

sections.title: WhatsApp Integration
sections.summary: WhatsApp integration via the Baileys unofficial bridge, covering QR code pairing, bot vs self-chat mode, session persistence, voice message transcription, and markdown conversion for WhatsApp formatting.

sections.title: Yuanbao
sections.summary: Connect Hermes to Yuanbao(https://yuanbao.tencent.com/), Tencent's enterprise messaging platform. The adapter uses a WebSocket gateway for real-time message delivery and supports both direct (C2C) and group conversations.

sections.title: G0DM0D3 — Godmode Jailbreaking
sections.summary: Bypass safety filters on API-served LLMs using techniques from G0DM0D3(https://github.com/elder-plinius/G0DM0D3) and L1B3RT4S(https://github.com/elder-plinius/L1B3RT4S) by Pliny the Prompter(https://x.com/elder_plinius) (elder-plinius).

sections.title: Google Workspace — Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Sheets & Docs
sections.summary: Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs integration for Hermes. Uses OAuth2 with automatic token refresh.
